comments.text: Awful vanilla commentary as if the place was sheer perfection. It was the same kind of commentary used in the video on Sweden and some of the photos of people are the same. comments.author: @djcallies
comments.text: This video made me cry. My family was stationed in Terceira a good portion of my life and although I am not Portuguese my heart is. I was never happier at anytime in my life then when I was there. I dream of moving back.

comments.author: @alvaropereira7922
comments.text: Paradise . This is where I was born . Left in 1985 to the states but always returned for vacations and to see my parents and family. The Azores is always in my heart.  Some day i will have to sell my houses and that day will be a difficult one . I hope my children  want to continue the attachment that i give them .  I know they do because when i mention that i will want to sell they push back .  I left the Azores but the Azores never left me.

comments.author: @NexivoStories
comments.text: In the 1970's there were Ships that took us from one island to another. Santo Amaro, Espirito Santo, Terra Alta, Ponta Delgada. I know that " Terra Alta " burned down. Santo Amaro was sold to an American. Espirito Santo, I had just disembarked in Sao Jorge , and as it was backing up it sunk. They fixed it but I was no longer living there. Is Ponta Delgada still around? It was a Ship that was sold by the Portuguese Navy.
